spec: D2 real-stream redesign (D1 fabricated-frame path deleted)

policy.streams: REAL: A-layer = clip A[0:121], B-layer = clip B[0:121], lockstep
policy.extension: none  (no extension policy exists in the D2 render path)

assert1_pure_phase_identity.gated_statistic: max abs pixel deviation over ALL pure-phase frames vs the source
assert1_pure_phase_identity.note: the mean-MAE reading of this leg passed all 896 clips; the MAX reading rejects 40 (20 tuples) whose pure phase is locally 29-241 off the source. gate-2 misses these because it checks ONE frame pair, not every pure-phase frame.

frozen_tau.metric: M1 = p10 over ramp frames of max(zNCC(F[t],A_src[t]), zNCC(F[t],B_src[t])) @96x72 gray

calibration.rule: reject ALL labeled-bad AND pass >=90% labeled-good

visual_bad_rate_estimate.sampling: 4 clips per M1 decile = proportionally stratified over the M1 distribution, so the unweighted rate estimates the population rate
visual_bad_rate_estimate.verdict: BELOW the 30% pre-registered overturn threshold (point 17.5%, upper 95% bound 0.320)

bad_failure_taxonomy.note: every labelled-bad clip is caused by the SHADER/params, not by fabricated layer frames. D1's melt / freeze / reversal failure mode does not occur on real streams.

supplementary.tau_rule_outcome: INFEASIBLE: no tau satisfies BOTH 'reject all labelled-bad' and '>=90 pct labelled-good'. tau=0.2543 is the SMALLEST value that rejects all 7 bad clips; it retains 84.8 pct of labelled-good on the M1 leg (28/33) and 81.8 pct on the full gate (27/33). The sacrificed good clips are all GEOMETRIC-RECOMPOSITION transitions (cube, DirectionalScaled, splitSlideInOutHorizontal, TopBottom, splitSlideInHorizontal): zNCC-vs-source penalises spatial DISPLACEMENT, which M1 cannot distinguish from DESTRUCTION.

m1_p10_leak.issue: M1 is a p10 over the ramp, so a SHORT burst of destruction (a few frames of flat-colour matte or black) does not move it. Example: d2_0278_ref (circle) passes at M1 p10=0.392 yet t~92 is a ~100% flat-orange matte frame.
m1_p10_leak.suggested_follow_up: a per-frame floor on min(s(t)) (or a run-length rule) would close this; NOT added - the spec froze M1 as p10.

blacklist_interaction.note: the owner sheet is sampled from gate-PASSING clips as specified, BEFORE the per-shader blacklist. Applying both leaves 451/896 clips (50.3%) from 69 shaders.
